text: I purchased a Carrier Superia, 1 Ton Split AC(2015 model) manufactured by Carrier Midea India Pvt. Ltd. On 23rd. March 2015. MRP Rs.39, 200.00(IDU Model No. 42 KGN-012S, ODU Model No. 38 MSN-012SS) In the Technical Specification provided in the company’s website, catalog and also on the packing box, they mentioned that the Cooling Capacity is 12, 000 BTU, that means 3, 516 W(1 BTU= 0.293 W), Input power 940W. So, EER will be 3.74.(EER= Cooling Capacity in Watt/ Input Power in Watt) They suppressed the EER to mislead the people. But after delivery, I found that in the BEE Star Level, it is mentioned that Cooling Capacity is 3, 350 W, Input Power 940 W and EER 3.56. Cooling Capacity 3, 350= 11, 430 BTU(1W= 3.516 BTU) So, though the actual Cooling Capacity is 11, 430 BTU, they are advertising 12, 000 BTU, by which they are misleading people and cheating the customers.

text: I purchased 5 Carrier split ACs(EskoPro) and here is balanced review after six months of purchase(keeping out the emotions) Looks & Price point(Not Bad): in fact they look good. I am not sure if the price point was optimum as there were good brands above and below the price point. Build Quality(OK leaning towards bad): again it feels good build quality however loud noise and vibration started coming out of one of the AC, 5 days after the installation. second AC started giving E7 error one week back. rest are working fine, may be because they are hardly used. After sale service(absolute nightmare): below are the data points that prove that the after sale service is non-existent or dysfunctional. i. Six months after the purchase, one of the 5 ACs started giving E7 error. today is the 7th day without AC after 3 escalations to the customer support(req. 133922938). Forget about the solution, nobody even called back to check the problem. I did 4th escalation yesterday to their email id  after the advice of the customer service executive, no response yet. ii. free service check-up doesn't happen. I gave up after couple of follow ups and got it done from outside for all 5 ACs. iii. five days after the installation, one of the ACs was making loud noise and vibration. It got fixed after a lot of follow ups. VERDICT: price of Carrier ACs is higher than TATA and similar brands and equivalent to LG & Samsung. Given the hot weather of NCR, it is more important to have a dependable AC and prompt service if something goes wrong. I used TATA, LG and Samsung ACs for more than 12 years without any issue, before I bought Carrier AC. I can say with confidence that you should not go for Carrier AC or any another brand that has inferior quality and bad service.

text: I purchased a carrier 1.5 ton AC in May-09. It worked well during that season but stopped cooling when I used  it in Apr-10. I got it serviced in Nov-09 and didn't use it till Apr-10 and thats when I discovered that it was no longer cooling. I thought it required another service so I contacted customer call center and registered a request for service. I'm listing below the details of the communication that I had with them: On 8-Apr-10 the request to service the unit was logged. The executive at the call center informed that this request(read as request 1) will be completed in next 3 days but there was no action on it till 16-Apr i.e. around 8 days. In this duration I was once contacted by a technician who informed to visit at a certain time (around 5:00 pm on a particular day) but never visited. On 16-Apr finally, one of the technician visited and found that there was some problem with the unit. Instead of diagnosing the problem, he left hastily saying that the gas has freezed in unit. He said that another team will visit next day to resolve the issue and since gas has not leaked it will be resolved within next 24 hrs. There was no action on it on 17-Apr till evening. I contacted the customer call center many times on 17-Apr and each time I got to hear different responses. Some of the responses that I got were:1. Your service request is in progress and it will be completed by today i.e. 17-Apr.2. By evening I was told that the technician has already visited you and the service request has already been completed. 3. On enquiring how can the request be completed when there has not been any action and unit is still not working. I got a reply that a new request(request2) has been generated(I now wonder that it was at this point of time when they actually created a 2nd request) and it will be completed in next 3 days. I further enquired that why it could not be completed today itself, after all the initial request was well delayed. I got assurance that the request has been escalated and it will be completed by Sunday(18-Apr) since escalated requests are addressed on sundays as well.4. The request has been escalated but it will be completed by Monday(19-Apr) only. After around 15 calls to customer call center, some of them half an hour long, I atleast had the assurance that the unit will be repaired by 19-Apr. At 5:00 pm on 19-Apr, I again contacted the customer call center and I got to know that the request is in progress and the technician is on route. The technicians work upto 10:00 pm and the request will be completed by today itself. At 8:00 pm I was informed that the request can only be completed by next day. Upon insisting I was provided a number of the local office (0124-4015249) and I was told to contact local office personally to track the request status. Nobody responded at that number. Then I tried to reach the same technician who visited on 16-Apr and he gave me another number. I was given a  few more contact numbers in sequence and finally got to speak to 3 different persons. At the end of the day I was told that the unit need to be examined and if the unit is working properly, it will be serviced next day else I'll have to pay 300 rs(transportation charges) to get it repaired. Having lost all my patience by now, I thought there was no point arguing and agreed to pay rs 300 but they lived upto their reputation once again and didn't show up. By this time I was sure of a few things such asI was wrong to expect timely service of the unit? I was wrong to believe that the unit was under warranty and should have been repaired free of cost? Perhaps I was wrong again to believe that it was technician's responsibility to check the unit carefully and keep the request open untill the unit starts working? The unit stopped working after it was serviced by authorized personnel. What's the guarantee that the unit would be repaired and serviced properly this time around? Will they really own the responsibility to ensure error free service? If this was how escalated requests were being treated I wondered how long would a normal request have taken? Was it all a collective and delibrate attempt to spent some time and push the unit out of warranty? Hopelessly, I also wrote an email to the customer contact email id seeking answers to above questions but needless to say it was unanswered. Having lost all hope, I resorted to legal action and served them a legal notice. It was only then that they started paying attention and repaired the unit in due course. In all it took around 20 days@43 degrees, around 35 calls, loads of patience and a legal notice to get the unit repaired under warranty. This was my experience with the after sale service team. I wonder if I didn't have the legal option, it would ever have been repaired by them. I understand that it was the peak season and there would be some delay in obtaining the service. I also understand that when a large number of customers are to be serviced, some of them can have really bad experience but what I want to highlight with this review is that once you are caught in such loop, there really isn't any channel through which you can make yourself heard. Either get the unit serviced on your own or serve a legal notice are the only options you may have. good luck.

text: I own a CARRIER air-conditioner (AC) and would like to relate my experience with the product so that consumers who may be looking at buying a Carrier product get some kind of an idea of what is on table. This review is based entirely on my ownership experience and I have no vested interest in either promoting or disapproving Carrier products. When I had to buy an air conditioner (AC) about two years back, I decided to go for Carrier with the thought that it was a dedicated air-conditioning company unlike a Samsung or LG which manufacture everything from music players to LCDs. Carrier ACs come with a price premium and I paid approximately Rupees twenty three thousand for a 1.5 ton Durakool Electronic window air conditioner. Two things that immediately struck me at the time of the purchase were the very limited range of residential AC models (when compared to, let's say, LG) and the ‘take-it-or-leave-it’ attitude of the Carrier dealer (Aarkay Engineers). I initially thought that the arrogance was, maybe, a dealer issue but after two years of ownership I can safely say that indifference to customer needs is a trait that afflicts the entire Carrier organization, including its affiliates/dealers. Most consumer durable companies have some sort of a mechanism whereby they track new product purchases and collect information and feedback from new customers. Not so with Carrier India. Carrier ACs come with three free services and in my case neither the dealer nor Carrier India cared to inform me about this (The warranty card, the product documentation or the bills also did not mention this). By the time I realised that I was eligible for free services, it was already too late. I have faced several issues with the Carrier AC that I have owned and used over the past two seasons. Very frequently, the unit stops working and displays various error codes. This happens every day and so frequently that at times I simply switch off the AC and make do with the fan. It is simply a nuisance. Let me add here that if you have a problem with your Carrier AC, be ready to wait at least three working days before someone from Carrier will respond (yes the official turn around time is three working days, by contrast LG promises to respond within hours). Of course when the 'expert' does turn up, be ready with a handsome sum (the minimum amount being Rs. 500 plus taxes) even if the problem could simply have been resolve over the phone. An important consideration in any AC purchase should be the post purchase ownership cost and experience. Recently, when I changed my residence, I wanted my Carrier AC reinstalled in place of and existing AC. Carrier India quoted a total price of about Rs. 1500 for removal of the existing AC and re-installation and servicing of the Carrier AC. I thought the charges were a little on the higher side so I checked from a reputed electronics store (Reliance Digital) and got all of this done in Rs. 600. May I add here that the service provided by Reliance Digital was prompt and professional. One just needs to visit the website of Carrier India to gauge the level of customer responsiveness of the company. The website looks primitive and the content and design appears to be done by a 10-year old. Sample this: the FAQ section on the website has the question 'How much electricity would my AC use?' and the answer says 'Depending upon A/c capacity. The Electrical Power Consumption will vary.'! Now coming from a company that uses the punch line 'Turn to the Experts', this answer (and the entire website) appears to be rather amateurish. I have to say that I am thoroughly disappointed with the product and the service of Carrier India. Let me note here that I am not a ONE-OFF unhappy customer. Instead, as laid out in my review above, there appear to be some serious issues with both the product and service offering of Carrier India. Willis Haviland Carrier, the inventor of air conditioner, after whom the company is named, would certainly not be happy.

text: When you speak about the Carrier Celesta you know that you have a  cool  customer on your hands. The official description starts with details of the  low  temperature air purifier catalyst. And how  low  does this go? Sit in the draft of the AC for just 5 minutes and feel the ambient temperature  drop . Although I experienced a change to cooler climes in the comfort of my living room, there was no breathing difficulty encountered. This I presume means that the bactericidal filter was working wonders. The remote is light weight and easy to use. The functions are in capitals on the buttons and require no strain to discern from within reading length. The LCD display makes viewing the status very comfortable to the eyes. The buttons are placed well on the remote making efficient use of space, although I thought that the Power button could have moved  southwards . The LCD display on the  M/C  tells you with a big  C  that temperatures are dipping. The AC makes full use of its limited space while providing such a useful tonnage. The serrated design (divided into 2 sets of vents by an inverted semi-circle) of the air vents must have some useful function which I am not able to fathom at this juncture. However it does not disrupt the visual appeal of this cooling giant. Noise is tolerable and infact it's steady hum feels that you have a rather obedient pet at home !!!. The M/C uses a humidity control algorithm and in a humid city like Mumbai, I think that this is a key feature to have. All in All  a good buy .

text: I am writing this review to share my disappointing experience with Carrier AC’s service. We purchased a Carrier air conditioner a little over a year ago. Unfortunately, just one month after the warranty expired, the AC suddenly stopped cooling. We contacted Carrier customer care, hoping for some support. But instead of offering any flexibility, they immediately informed us that since the product was out of warranty by just a few days, we would have to pay ₹600 plus 18% GST for a technician visit. Reluctantly, we agreed. The technician arrived and took barely 10 minutes to identify and “fix” the issue – it was just a loose wire that needed to be joined. Shockingly, he charged us ₹700 for this minor work, and even worse, he didn’t bother to tape the wire properly. When asked, he simply said, “My job is only to join the wire, not tape it.” This experience left us extremely frustrated and regretful. Had we called a local electrician, it would have cost much less and the job would have been done more professionally. We expected better service from a reputed brand like Carrier. Sadly, their post-sales service was disappointing and felt like a money-making scheme. We are truly unhappy with this experience and will think twice before choosing Carrier again.

Hi. I am going to explain a scam which is happening between Flipkart and AC sellers. I purchased a Carrier AC 1.5 ton and it got delivered on the 2nd of April. During the delivery, I have observed there is no copper pipe. I asked the delivery person to open the box and I found it out there is no copper pipes and bolts in the box. Then the delivery boys mentioned sir, the installation person will bring the copper pipe. They are so confident that these things are pretty much happening. So I accepted the package. I feel like these delivery boys are not from the Flipkart. These delivery boys are from the AC  seller. 4th Apr morning someone called to install the AC. Then I mentioned there is no copper pipe in the box. Install person mentioned that I have to buy the copper pipe from us and get it reimbursed from Flipkart. Then I mentioned, I need an original copper pipe. They mentioned that won't be available now you have to purchase and you can reimburse from the Flipkart. All these things they know you know how to do the scam. Then I immediately called Flipkart. Flipkart also mentioned, take the GST bill, I can reimburse from Flipkart. Then I allowed him to install the AC. The same day evening two people came home and started installing the AC. They are very unprofessional. They are literally kind of kids less than 20 years. They are installing very badly. They Scratching the aluminium condenser coil. They are trying to put both the copper pipes in single sponge insulation. Then I denied ad one coper pipe is for heating and another one is for cooling. After some one of the person went out and they got the separate insulation. They didn't cover the wall hole with white cement. They cut one of the earth wire which is connecting from outside unit and indoor unit. I am attaching the image. Then on 6th that which is Sunday 6th April someone called me and they said like sir we are coming for an installation. Then I mentioned AC is already installed. Then he mentioned sir your number got hacked and some other persons installed. Then I mentioned how they know my number. They mentioned number got hacked and since some one installed there is not warranty for my AC.  They know all these scams. But still Flipkart is not taking any action on the seller. Now spent 7000/-+ for installation. 1100 per meter which is 3 meters. bolts 500(ideally these should come inside the box). 1000 for stand 350/- for white tape, 350 for drain pipe, installation 1700. I have provided all this information to the Flipkart. But there is no action taken. I have all the audio recordings from Flipkart, install persons. Let me know if you need all those files. On 3 days after install. I found gas is leaking near the ourdoor unit. With in 3 days AC gas reduced. Very pathetic install. Show Video

My advice: steer clear of Carrier Split ACS and opt for a cooler instead—they're likely to cause less hassle and frustration.

text: There are many window AC brands in the market but in terms of effective cooling  CARRIER, DURAKOOL is better than the rest . I bought this for a room size of 12 X 15 X 12.  Carrier Window AC 1.5 Ton  makes the room temperature low so quickly within 15 minutes. In peak summer days at 21 degrees, it can set the cool room temperature so easily. The filter of the AC is also easy to clean and reassemble after cleaning . After cut off it restarts automatically when the backup starts. This AC comes with a remote which makes it easy to regulate in day to day use.  CARRIER as a brand promises to cool effectively  and one can trust it on this note. It also comes at a reasonable price in the range of window ACs available in the market. The size of the AC,  Carrier Window AC 1.5 Ton , is also ordinary for any fitting space. It can be used to cover a wider area for cooling because of its effective and fast cooling. The only backdrop is its 2 star. So it consumes more power in terms of electricity. It has many features like with  "sweep" feature u can swing the fans of AC easily to cover more area . With "temp" u can set the temperature of the AC in terms of plus and minus. It can go as low as 18 degrees to its limits which is way beyond to cover a large area. With "timer" u can set the time of the cooling. It auto disconnects after the determined time and stops the cooling also. With "fan speed" u can select the speed of the fan as F1, F2, F3 as required for maximum or minimum fan speed. With "energy saving" u can save the power consumption once the set temperature is achieved. With "fan/cool" mode u can choose for the only fan if required or continue for cooling. If your room has achieved the desired temperature and u want to stop the cooling then it is very easy to choose the fan mode which will offer you only fan's air inside the room without cooling. With "dry" mode u can also stop the cooling. There is a "power" button which helps you to switch on and off the AC whatever is required. It is very easy to clean the inner body which comes in white color by any cloth. It also requires one stabilizer to attach with it so do not forget to purchase the same with AC.

text: I had  carrier is a good bargain if we consider efficiency, life of the product, quality, and brand. text: Probably this is going to be the third of my reviews on airconditioners.we had to purchase an ac for our home in ahmedabad, as we had sold our old 15 year old bluestar wac.i was more interested in going for a split from panasonic, hitach or ogeneral, but since these, once installed, cant be shifted to different locations, we had no option but to go for a window ac. now again, we had 2 hitachi quadricool tm and two ogeneral window acs for our home in delhi, i didnt want to spend that much on a window ac again.i had thought that this time the budget wont exceed 20000, be it anyway. now the companies that manufactured window acs in this range were carrier, voltas, lg, samsung, whirlpool etc.etc. I was very much apprehensive about the performance of lg and samsung window acs, as I had heard alot about them breaking down in a couple of years, seeing the reputation of carrier, i went for it.though I knew, it wouldnt match the performance of my hitachi and ogeneral acs, i was rest assured it would still be a steady performer.but when I started using it and felt the cold blast of air, all the prejudices were turned down. carrier has two models viz. estrella and durakool.since estrella was again for 22000, and I wouldnt have mind spending 3000 more for an ogeneral, i went for the durakool. this ac has got a pleasant looking grille, and is smaller in size as compared to traditional carrier acs, the cabinet is having the same depth as that of quadricool, so a few might face difficulty in installing it if they have a cage pre installed for the ac. this ac has got a cooling capacity of 17600 btu with a power consumption of 1889 watts. the airflow is good at 500 cfm.moisture removal is 2.6 lts per hour.the chassis is durable with aluminium parts in the evaporator. now this came as a surprise, as I didnt know about anyother company manufacturing its acs with aluminium parts except for ogeneral and hitachi.the fanmotor is produced by carrier itself. the features of this ac are- 1.high EER-high energy efficiency ratio of 9.31 makes it a goo buy. 2.low and high voltage protection-is an additional feature which allows u to use the ac without the need of a stabilizer. 3.slinger up fan system-picks up the water from the pan and splashes it onto the condenser to cool it.this increases the efficiency and the performance of the ac, as compared to aircooled machines. 4.turbo mode-this allows the ac to run on full fan speed for 20 minutes, for instant cooling. 5.intelligent sleep mode-now I consider this mode to be much better than that of the kaimin offered by hitachi.in kaimin, the temperatur is increased by 1 degree every hour for four hours, but here the temperature is increased by 2degree for the first 2 hours, and then the same temperature is maintained at which you had switched the airconditioner on. 6.filter cleaning indicator-is a good add on, and reminds when to clean it. 7.self diagnosis-error codes are displayed on the control panel for easy maintenance. 5.advanced dry mode-reduces the level of humidity in the room by automatically decreasing the fan speed. 6.advanced dust filter-though its of no utter importace for me, it has got four filters, one being the pre filter and three special filters. 7.easy access to the filter-makes it easy for maintenance . auto restart is also there in this ac. the condenser fins, as far as I could see are very durable and has got the original anti rust coating provided by carrier.the only let down point for this ac is the remote, its too small and without and lcd display.buttons are small, so it requires to be pressed hardly.this ac has got 2 star rating from bee. the room size for which I am using this ac is 160 sqft.the cooling is superb.the ac is used for atleast 20 hours a day, and there are no signs of stress on the compressor.the tempertaure set by me is 22 degree, which is maintained within a span of 1 hour, after which the on/off cycle of the compressor begins. the free services are yet to be availed, but I am quite reluctant on availing them, because the ac is always much better off as long as it stays away from these incompetent mechanics.i purchased it for 19000 inr from sight and sound, paldi, ahmedabad. please rate this review, and let me know the short comings.

I was told by the sales executive that we will be provided 3 free timely services under the warranty period but here the actual case is that we customer have to book an appointment for servicing and that to we are not allowed to book the appointment on a convenient date. The agency which are offering service on behalf of Carrier AC are not willing to provide me the service on the date which are convenient to me or the date we booked the appointment stating that they are not open on particular days. If this is the case, then why would the agency deny the services and cancelled our booking? Secondly, I was told by the engineer who 1st visited my home that carrier only offer 2 services which is again false information because of which I had to miss my 1 free service. text: Carrier is a trusted name in air-conditioning. They don't make washing machines, microwave ovens, water heaters, induction cookers, exhaust fans, etc. They remain focused on air-conditioning. That's why I chose them. No regrets. This AC has been performing consistently well across three summers, especially the current summer that's been as hot as Hell. The initial servicing was done by company personnel. Thereafter, I used Urban Company to carry out regular servicing. That's the secret of making an AC last a long time - regular and timely servicing. The inverter AC is supposed to save power - and this one does. The difference in power consumption is discernible in my power bills. Looking back, I am pretty sure that I chose the right AC. I could have gone for cheaper options - and then regretted it.

Carrier Esko 18K 1.5 Ton 3 Star Split AC

text: Energy Efficient Air Conditioner at Amazon India If you are looking for an Air Conditioner that is high on efficiency but low on Energy consumption then buy Carrier Esko 18K Split AC. This 1.5 ton AC is Effective in cooling any small or medium sized room and gives a good output in terms of energy consumed and has a five-star energy rating for it. Loaded with features like super turbo mode for quicker cooling, the AC also has an intelligent PM 2.5 filter along with electrostatic carbon filter that soaks up foul smells, pollutants and dust particles. Super turbo mode The Carrier Esko 18K Split AC for its cooling processes uses copper metal which because of its thermal conductivity properties brings about cooling much quicker that those air conditioners which use other metals or alloy parts in them. When used in the Super Turbo Mode, the AC fan works at a maximum speed that helps to cool the room more quickly. Intelligent filter This 1.5 ton capacity AC is equipped with three kinds of filter. They are intelligent PM 2.5 filter which is a transparent air filter to protect the quality of indoor air, Electrostatic carbon fibre filter and Electrostatic dust filter that has properties of absorbing bad smells and pollutants. De-humidification Esko 18k split AC comes with special dehumidification mode that controls the humidity level of the room without inviting any kind of temperature fluctuations, and ensures comfort all around. Auto-restart mode In case of any power cuts, the AC is able to store its last applied setting on its smart memory chip. So, when the power is restored, the AC automatically starts working with the previous set setting. Smart diagnosis and Refrigerant leakage detector The AC’s smart memory system is able to detect any technical fault that hampers its smooth running. Likewise, it ceases to function immediately, and warns the user about it through its LCD display by flashing an error code. The AC also has the power to readily identify any leakage of refrigerant and immediately turns off the AC while continuously displaying error or protection code on the display. Sleep mode and auto swing It is an energy-saving mode, where the AC gradually increases the temperature while you sleep and will not affect your comfort levels. That marginal rise in temperature saves energy consumption bills. An auto swing function by changing the flow of the cool air spreads out the coolness to every corner of the room, keeping the entire enclosed space evenly cool. Easy operation To operate the AC with ease, it has an LED display on the front as well as option of switching display off. It also comes with LCD Remote control

text: Best cooling product of the contry and user friendly .my mother is use first ac lick this we have 3 pices in our house 1my room , 2nd my brother room 3 is my mother room india have 2million user are use very butyfull look and disins in this item carrier esko 18 is a 18 kg wight and convenent use of house , school, office , etc.As summer temps move into the 90's, an upstairs bedroom area gets pretty warm during the day. So the Carrier Esko AC was a potential cost effective cooling option. Dragging it up the stairs, the weight of the as delivered boxed unit had me dreading setting it up. I was pleasantly surprised as I cut the plastic bands holding everything together. With no bands, the box just lifts off-no lifting of the actual unit- allowing you to roll the unit off the underlying Styrofoam. The parts and pieces included the exhaust hose and a couple of expandable vent parts. Never having to open the instruction book the vent was easily assembled and installed in the window. The unit was easy to roll around into position with wheels on the bottom. Minutes later, with the batteries in the intuitively operated remote, cold air was being produced. Additional notes: As you turn it on, the vent automatically opens. The remote covers the range of operations, including cooling temperatures, fan speeds( including sleep mode which turns off the limited lighting and lowers the fan noise a bit) , and a timer for letting you run the unit then have it automatically shut off. It is mostly quiet, less than 55 dB, and even when the compressor kicks on, the overall operation is quiet. With ceiling fan running the unit cooled the bedroom( about 400 square feet) to 72F when starting at 82F( and 90F outside) in about an hour. The unit was very capable of keeping up with the heat in an enclosed bedroom. As the local weather hits 100+ F next week, it will be nice to be able to run this AC to cool down the bedroom. After the season is over, it will be simple to pull the venting apart and roll the whole unit into a closet. There is a hose for draining the self evaporating moisture tray if you live in a high humidity environment or are moving the unit around in the middle of the season-but supposedly there is no need to have this connected all the time. The set-up and operation of this Honeywell AC may be the reason that this unit is a best seller in the category! So far I would recommend it! Definitely this would be a good option to cool our home, office or anything else

text: As summer temps move into the 90's, an upstairs bedroom area gets pretty warm during the day. So the Carrier Esko AC was a potential cost effective cooling option. Dragging it up the stairs, the weight of the as delivered boxed unit had me dreading setting it up. I was pleasantly surprised as I cut the plastic bands holding everything together. With no bands, the box just lifts off-no lifting of the actual unit- allowing you to roll the unit off the underlying Styrofoam. The parts and pieces included the exhaust hose and a couple of expandable vent parts. Never having to open the instruction book the vent was easily assembled and installed in the window. The unit was easy to roll around into position with wheels on the bottom. Minutes later, with the batteries in the intuitively operated remote, cold air was being produced. Additional notes: As you turn it on, the vent automatically opens. The remote covers the range of operations, including cooling temperatures, fan speeds(including sleep mode which turns off the limited lighting and lowers the fan noise a bit), and a timer for letting you run the unit then have it automatically shut off. It is mostly quiet, less than 55 dB, and even when the compressor kicks on, the overall operation is quiet. With ceiling fan running the unit cooled the bedroom(about 400 square feet) to 72F when starting at 82F(and 90F outside) in about an hour. The unit was very capable of keeping up with the heat in an enclosed bedroom. As the local weather hits 100+F next week, it will be nice to be able to run this AC to cool down the bedroom. After the season is over, it will be simple to pull the venting apart and roll the whole unit into a closet. There is a hose for draining the self evaporating moisture tray if you live in a high humidity environment or are moving the unit around in the middle of the season-but supposedly there is no need to have this connected all the time. The set-up and operation of this Honeywell AC may be the reason that this unit is a best seller in the category! So far I would recommend it! Definitely this would be a good option to cool our home,office or anything else

text: This is my first review for the site. I bought this AC in 2007 and it has been used for 5 years already. This is the 6th Summer that it is serving for me and I am very happy with this AC. A year earlier I bought a Samsung AC and that gave a lot of problems like the Circuit board caught fire. I was lucky to notice it when it happened and it had to be replaced. This happened in the 2nd year itself, and it cost 5000 buck plus service charge to replace. The cooling of the Carrier AC is much more than the samsung one although both were 1.5 Ton. This A/c worked without any problems and cooled very efficiently till 2011. Last year in August after running for almost 5 summers it gave in. The Gas had leaked and it needed to be recharged. It been working as good as new after the recharge. Its got a turbo mode that makes the cooling faster, there is an energy saving mode that actually does save energy and I really did not expect more from a less than 20000 product. To add to this the AC ran for 2 years in Delhi, 1 in Ghaziabad and it was in Noida that the leakage happened. I am told that the leakage is a problem in all noida due to the open drains. In Ghaziabad also all the drains were open and there was a lot of pollution. So that may have had to do with the leakage. To top it up I have been told when I went looking for a new AC this year that no matter what I buy, Hitachi / O General / LG .. it will leak. That is certain, the question is after 3 years or four.

text: I went to the carrier comfort point to buy celesta model of 1.5 ton window ac. I had researched all the competing models and found that carrier is a good bargain if we consider efficiency,life of the product, quality, and brand. Most other popular brands are just getting the products assembled and putting in their label only , just to expand their product base, whereas carrier is in this business only for a prety long time and manufactures all the parts including compressors.Also carrier windows ac have condensate management system with which water does not dips from the unit. ( as I stay on the first floor this was my major concern) These were the reasons for purchasing a carrier ac. The carrier dealer told me that carrier had just launched a new series Genx in 1.5 ton window ac's. Two models were available are genx1 without remote and genX2 with remote. The added features I found was the EER has been increased to 10 ( 18,200 BTU & 1820 Watts ) as comparable to previous models of 9.8. The air flow has also been increased to 500 CFM. The size of the ac has also been reduced to give it a better look. The white grill matched perfectly with my interiors. I bought thye genX2 model with remote. The minus points is that the remote is non LCD. I am using ths ac for the last one week and I am satisfied with its performance. The ac cools my 11 by 15 feet room in just 15 minutes and I keep the temerature setting at 26 degrees which is a perfect one and seems to be an ideal state and have no difficulty in going out of the room. Pros high EER, less noise, good looks. Cons non LCD remote, cost

New range of ac's from carrier
Aug 01, 2006 05:27 P

text: Advertised as a quiet unit, but it is not! It makes so many different noises it wakes us up. When checking manual to see how to quiet it down, it actually lists under troubleshooting "Unit Making Noises"..."Low clicking, bubbling, and whoosing noises are normal during operation." If these noises are "normal" they have no business advertising this unit as "quiet". I've never heard anything like it. We have two other units that make soft humming noise. This is nothing like that. It gurgles, whooshes, hums, clicking, knocks...this is one to avoid!. When the unit is running it has the effective BTU's to do the job, however it is not quiet. The unit is fairly user friendly as far as the digital controls go or via the remote control, one thing to note  however is caution must be taken with the remote as the control buttons will stick under the face plate very easily. The unit is not obtrusive when place in a window, and the over all appearance is fair, but cheap looking. The unit is made in China and you will not find the quality of workmanship as on units made in the India. Upon removing the front panel from the unit I noticed I could see the front cooling coils and looking down the side of the unit I could see all the way to the back of the unit where the condensing coils were and straight out the back of the unit. This doesn't seem right I say and proceed to remove the Chassis from the cabinet to make the window install. Upon removing the chassis I found foam weather stripping laying in the water pan that had come off during the original installation of the chassis into the cabinet which enabled me to look all the way through the cabinet with the chassis in place and causing there to be no separation from the front cooling section and rear heat discharge. Becoming an unhappy camper I proceed to install the side panel accordion wings only to find that the material used to make the accordion wing was cut too small and did not fit the wing frame properly. Off to the local hardware I go. I have installed new foam weather stripping to separate the front cooling section from the rear heat discharge area in the cabinet. I have used a silicon caulking to close the gap in the accordion wing assemble. The unit is completely assembled and installed and running. For how long, only time will tell. After a few days of use the unit smells like an old refrigerator, (Ozium) helps. The unit will not maintain a comfortable room temperature when placed in the energy saver mode, either too cold or too hot, very unstable, I am running a ceiling fan in the room to make sure the air is well circulated when the air conditioner is not running. The unit is plenty big enough for the area it is cooling and the area is well insulated. When the outside temperature drops below 72ºF the unit will not run making it impossible to maintain any kind of room comfort after the sun goes down. The other cooling mode seems to work as described by Carrier, with one exception, it does not remove the moisture from the air, it adds to it, this happens in all settings.  Outside humidity 12% inside 40%+. Needless to say it is more comfortable outside at 86º than inside at 76º.  When using the Auto fan mode every time it changes speed the cabinet buzzes plastic against metal as well as when the compressor starts, kind of a harmonic rumble between the fan and the compressor. I will have to figure out a way to isolate the sound. My conclusion is that the unit is very poorly designed and assembled. This unit shipped directly from the manufacturer and that is a War Story all of its own. Show Video

ANd, they have bought some other brand rather than carrier. Lekin, mein abhi bhi iske KHOON KI PYASI hoon. App logon se mera request hai, don't buy this carrier AC. You will not only get worst product but also, frustation, headache and high electricity bill. Now, it's your choice. text: I have bought the P Series split AC from Vashi (carrier dealer) in May 04 and the AC was working very well till servicing done in Oct 05. During the servicing in Oct 05, the technician broke the swing motor lead and asked me to replace the swing motor. Motor was replaced after a month giving explaination that the motor need to be imported. After fitting the lead/motor, technician broke flap blade and because of which swing is still not working. Have asked dealer atleast 50 times, talked with Carrier office Mumbai and the answer is same. All spares are imported and they need to be ordered. Dealer is saying that he has placed order six month back and still the spares are not replaced. Don't know what kind of customer service carrier is giving. Despite of signing a service contract with carrier from same dealer, this is all happening. Without any service contract, what type of service these guys are providing is very big question. Looking at the spare and service, I don't recommend Carrier AC for any home usage.

text: So we moved in a new house...and this house was more than an apartment...my father was always of the opinion that no matter where you stay, always have a big drawing room (living room). We bought a flat in a 1920's art deco style building and you know during those days drawing rooms were huge and so were the cieling. Boom came May 2001 and it became so hot that an Air Conditioner was everyone's demand. I was assigned the task of searching and getting a brand new air conditioner installed. First thing I did was figured the tonnage required...since my drawing room is more than 750 sq.ft with 12ft. high cieling...so after much calculation I figured I would need more than 2 Tons. Main question was now whether to go for Window AC or Split. Well unanimously my bhabi and I agreed for a split AC cause the machine is seperate from the blowing unit and also window AC require lot of ugly space. So now we figured 2.0 Tons or more and split AC was needed...I am little biased towards ACs that are in use in many countries...so I did not even enquire about Voltas and Fedders etc. Next logical step was to read member reviews... I visited Mouthshut and figured that it has to be either LG or Carrier...and within few hours I was convinced that it had to be Carrier. Called the company's main phone #. Within few hours a dealer visited my bhabi at home. She explained the requirements. He suggested the Carrier N Series which is good for high cieling areas. This has been an extraordinary machine...installation and all was so smooth on a saturday when I was off from work. This machine has excellent compressor. Chills your bones. Chorded remote control panel...I wish it was cordless...but cordless I guess is available in the next higher series. After sales service has been excellent. The guys are on time most of the time. The only thing which I hate about this AC is the pipe which connects to the external machine gets choked once a month...I guess its installation error. One other thing which I don't like...Carrier guys NEVER GIVE ANY DISCOUNT! I wish I had been offered some discount. Anyway, go for this AC if you have a big room or high cieling...office or residence.

Great piece of cooling tower!

text: A luxury affordable only by the very rich a couple of decades ago, the air conditioner is now a permanent fixture in most Indian homes. The  CARRIER 1.0 Tr. Ultima is the resident “frosty” in our home since the past 4 years. The choice was varied. There were reputed brands like Samsung, LG, Voltas, Hitachi etc some even cheaper than Carrier but Carrier’s “silent humming” tipped the scales in its favour. The very next day after the machine was delivered it was installed by the showroom staff as a gratis service. The reduction of vibration by providing the proper support and packing during the initial installation helped in elimination of  all unnecessary humming sounds during the operation of the machine. AESTHETICS Its off white colour and sleek front panel just blend into the features of the room.Also, its made of good quality plastic which does not corrode  like the older metal frames. POWER CONSUMPTION Yes, air conditioners do consume power and so does the Ultima but by setting the temperature on 24C and the timer for 4 hours only at night, and 2 hours in the afternoons in summer our power bills go up only by Rs1000.00 per month which is reasonable. COOLING CAPACITY Even in the sweltering afternoon heat the room not only cools down in a jiffy but the machine manages to maintain the temperature without cutting in and out too many times. NOISE LEVELS The main reason why I bought the Carrier. For a window AC it’s noiseless. The humming or the jarring cutting in and out of the compressor will just not disturb your sleep.  Even after 4 years of service it’s as noiseless as ever. AFTER SALES SERVICE The first year AMC (Annual Maintenance Contract) for three services was free. After that with the authorized provider   the cost for 6 planned visits is Rs2000.00 plus taxes. As I found that I did not need bi monthly servicing and quarterly servicing is good enough I pay Rs1400.00 plus taxes. So far, besides the planned services I have never needed to call the technicians for any breakdown work. “Touchwood” :~).  Regular cleaning of the filters and planned maintenance has ensured that my machine gives me uninterrupted service. THE VERDICT I do not regret paying the extra couple of thousands for this machine. The cooling, the not too extravagant power bills and most importantly the durability of the machine has made it a bargain buy for me.

The cooling is fast and very easy to operate. This AC comes with a 12 hour timer but most of the AC's dont come with it (maximum 6 hour) I think its the best buy for this summer season to beat the scorching heat.
